an1.268:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, there is no occasion for a person accomplished in right view to regard any *saṅkhāra* as permanent. This case does not exist.
an1.268:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, for an ordinary person to regard any *saṅkhāra* as permanent. This case does exist."
an1.269:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, there is no occasion for a person accomplished in right view to regard any *saṅkhāra* as *sukha*. This case does not exist.
an1.269:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, for an ordinary person to regard any *saṅkhāra* as *sukha*. This case does exist."
an1.270:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, there is no occasion for a person accomplished in right view to regard any *dhamma* as self. This case does not exist.
an1.270:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, for an ordinary person to regard any *dhamma* as self. This case does exist."
an1.271:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, there is no occasion for a person accomplished in right view to deprive their mother of life. This case does not exist.
an1.271:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, for an ordinary person to deprive their mother of life. This case does exist."
an1.272:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, there is no occasion for a person accomplished in right view to deprive their father of life. This case does not exist.
an1.272:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, for an ordinary person to deprive their father of life. This case does exist."
an1.273:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, there is no occasion for a person accomplished in right view to deprive an *arahant* of life. This case does not exist.
an1.273:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, for an ordinary person to deprive an *arahant* of life. This case does exist."
an1.274:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, there is no occasion for a person accomplished in right view, with a corrupted mind [*citta*], to draw blood from the Tathāgata. This case does not exist.
an1.274:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, for an ordinary person, with a corrupted mind [*citta*], to draw blood from the Tathāgata. This case does exist."
an1.275:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, there is no occasion for a person accomplished in right view to split the *Saṅgha*. This case does not exist.
an1.275:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, for an ordinary person to split the *Saṅgha*. This case does exist."
an1.276:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, there is no occasion for a person accomplished in right view to declare allegiance to another teacher. This case does not exist.
an1.276:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, for an ordinary person to declare allegiance to another teacher. This case does exist."
an1.277:1.1: "It is impossible, *bhikkhus*, there is no occasion for two *arahants* who are perfectly enlightened ones to arise simultaneously — neither before nor after — in a single world-system. This case does not exist.
an1.277:1.2: But it is possible, *bhikkhus*, for just one *arahant* who is a perfectly enlightened one to arise in a single world-system. This case does exist."
